ops.action: Add/adjust a UX regression test that asserts (1) exit code, (2) key stdout/stderr lines, and (3) a stable error message for failure paths. Prefer partial/regex assertions and avoid brittle full-output snapshots unless output is explicitly stable.
ops.title: Add CLI UX tests for new commands
ops.trigger: When adding or changing a CLI command/subcommand (new noun/verb, flags, output text, exit behavior)

ops.action: Add/update a subsystem README that explains: the user-facing commands, where data is stored, primary module responsibilities, and the quickest 'smoke path' to validate behavior.
ops.title: Document subsystem entrypoints and mental model
ops.trigger: When adding a new subsystem or expanding an existing one with multiple new modules (e.g., `core.py`, `models.py`, `cli.py`, `recall.py`)

ops.action: Update `.gitignore` to exclude the artifact(s) and add a short doc note describing where the artifact lives and how to reset it safely.
ops.title: Ignore new persistent artifacts in git
ops.trigger: When introducing any persistent local artifact (sqlite db, index, cache dir, logs, tmp files) as part of a new subsystem

ops.action: Run `uv run basedpyright` and fix all reported issues; treat LSP diagnostics as hints only (not a gate). Follow with `uv run ruff check .` before tests.
ops.title: Prefer basedpyright over LSP diagnostics
ops.trigger: When you see/typecheck errors via editor/LSP or are about to declare a Python change 'done'

ops.body:
# Ship New Subsystem Checklist

Use this when you add a new Loom subsystem or a major slice of one (new CLI noun/verb + new storage/indexing + multiple new modules).

## Procedure

1. Define the public contract
   - Decide the CLI shape (`loom <noun> <verb> ...`) and failure semantics (exit codes, stderr vs stdout).
   - Ensure there is a stable machine-readable mode when appropriate (e.g., `--json`).

2. Implement the core first
   - Put domain behavior in `src/agent_loom/<subsystem>/core.py` (or equivalent).
   - Keep side effects (I/O, printing) out of core where possible.

3. Add storage/indexing deliberately
   - Introduce models/types in `src/agent_loom/<subsystem>/models.py`.
   - If there is search/recall/indexing logic, isolate it in its own module (e.g., `recall.py`).
   - Decide where on disk persistent artifacts live; document reset semantics.

4. Wire the CLI entrypoints
   - Implement `src/agent_loom/<subsystem>/cli.py` as a thin layer over core.
   - Standardize error messages for common failure modes (missing args, not initialized, empty results).

5. Add docs at the subsystem level
   - Create/update `src/agent_loom/<subsystem>/README.md` with:
     - Purpose + mental model
     - Data locations
     - Key commands with examples
     - One "smoke path" to validate behavior

6. Add git hygiene
   - Update `.gitignore` for any new persistent artifacts (db/index/cache/tmp).

7. Add CLI UX regression tests
   - Add `tests/test_<subsystem>_cli_ux.py` that asserts:
     - Exit code
     - A few stable stdout/stderr lines (avoid brittle full snapshots)
     - At least one failure-path message

8. Run quality gates
   - `uv run ruff check .`
   - `uv run basedpyright`
   - `uv run pytest`

## Done Criteria
- Users can discover commands via `--help`, run a smoke path, and understand where data is stored.
- New persistent artifacts are ignored and documented.
- UX tests cover at least one happy path and one failure path.
- Ruff + basedpyright + pytest are clean.
ops.description: A short procedure for shipping a new Loom subsystem (core + CLI + storage + docs + UX tests) without leaving sharp edges.
ops.name: ship-new-subsystem-checklist
